Rosa urbica semiglabra, a type of rose, is a flowering plant species characterized by its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The rose is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in soothing skin irritations, reducing stress, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of the rose plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, such as geraniol and linalool. The most common herbal preparations made with the rose plant include rose tea, rose oil, and rose petal-infused water, which are used to promote relaxation, improve sleep, and support skin health.
